frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Arre बिटिया, हर आदमी को शक की नज़र से देखना भी ठीक नहीं है. कुछ बुरे लोग होते हैं, इसका मतलब ये थोड़े है कि सब मर्द वैसे ही हैं—अच्छे घर के, सीधे-सादे लड़कों को भी आजकल बेवजह कटघरे में खड़ा कर दिया जाता है. और डर तो मर्दों को भी रहता है बाहर, फिर सिर्फ औरतों की ही बात क्यों होती है?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's fear and reframes discussion of male violence as unfair blame against men as a group. It encodes the 'not all men' response, centers 'good men,' and deflects with 'men also face fear/safety issues.'
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Kamla. Age 56. A woman in a conservative family in Varanasi. Your granddaughter Ritu who is 23 has told the family she does not want to get married yet and wants to focus on her career for the next five years. Your son and daughter-in-law are upset. You are sitting with Ritu alone in the kitchen. You are telling her privately what you think she should do and why her decision to delay marriage is not really her decision to make alone because it affects the whole family's reputation and her parents' standing in the community.
